historical_note: This report was produced from the previous 350-row / 315-train / 35-eval artifacts. Preserve its metrics as historical only; do not compare directly to the current canonical 317-row artifacts.

artifacts.scan_summary: find . -path ./.venv -prune -o -path ./.git -prune -o -newer training/train_sft.py -type f -print → only reports/phase3_eval_smoke.json and the __pycache__ compile artifact.

eval_progress_bar_proof: 100%|██████████| 35/35 [00:15<00:00,  2.34it/s] → exactly 35 eval batches processed, matching 35 eval rows at per_device_eval_batch_size=1.
loss_interpretation: eval_loss=0.5844 is from a freshly initialized QLoRA + LoRA adapter (random init, no training). The number is NOT a quality signal — it only confirms that the forward pass completes end-to-end on every eval row without OOM. The actual eval loss after training will be measured once the adapter is trained.
observations: 35/35 eval batches processed at ~2.34 it/s on a 32 GiB RTX 5090; eval pass took 15.72 s end-to-end (forward only, no backward, no optimizer state).
Vram free after completion: 31.4 GiB / 32.6 GiB (within measurement noise of pre-run baseline) — no VRAM leak.

Output dir is empty (just the auto-created directory entry, 4096 bytes). No checkpoint, no adapter, no trainer_state, no tokenizer, no metrics file were written inside checkpoints/qwen-protesta-eval-smoke/.
run_eval_only never called trainer.train(); only trainer.evaluate() was invoked. safety_wiring_proof fields confirm this is structurally enforced.
max_seq_length kept at 20480; train and eval token distributions unchanged from the dry-run (overall_max=18916, eval_max=18910, eval_p95=7195).
trainable params = 40.37M / 7.66B (0.5273%) — identical to the 1-step smoke, confirming the LoRA wiring is stable.
Train dataset was loaded into SFTTrainer only because TRL requires train_dataset != None; it was never trained on (no optimizer.step() call, no gradients accumulated).
next_step_recommendation: Eval capacity is confirmed at per_device_eval_batch_size=1 with the longest eval row at 18,910 tokens. Safe to enable eval inside a future training run via eval_strategy='epoch' (or 'steps' + eval_steps=N) with per_device_eval_batch_size=1 — no OOM risk on a 32 GiB RTX 5090.
methodology_preserved: GPT-5.4-mini + validación humana Nico is the dataset origin; no gpt-5.5 baseline/origin claim added.
Historical 350-row dataset: all rows were treated as gold weight 1.0; no gold/silver split was introduced. Superseded by the canonical 317-row migration.
User message not duplicated: chat_formatted user content already embeds fecha+título+texto_original; this script does not re-concatenate.
max_seq_length kept at 20480 (Phase 3 readiness budget, overall_max=18916).
Eval schema, training schema, and codebook unchanged.

history.summary: 35/35 eval batches processed end-to-end at ~2.34 it/s; eval_loss=0.5844 (random-init LoRA, not a quality signal); VRAM fully released; output dir empty. The eval capacity gate is unblocked — per_device_eval_batch_size=1 fits the longest eval row (18,910 tokens) on a 32 GiB RTX 5090.
fixes_applied_for_eval_only_path: training/train_sft.py: added --eval-only + --eval-report CLI flags; documented in module docstring.
training/train_sft.py: extracted _build_model_and_tokenizer() helper so run_training and run_eval_only share one model-construction path.

training/train_sft.py: new run_eval_only() function. Gates in order: (1) validate_environment, (2) tokenizer + train+eval audit (FAIL LOUD before model load), (3) build model + trainer, (4) trainer.evaluate() exactly once. Never calls trainer.train().

training/train_sft.py: eval_cfg['no_save']=True, eval_strategy='no', per_device_eval_batch_size=1, save_strategy='no' all forced via build_sft_kwargs + belt-and-braces assertions inside run_eval_only.

scripts/test_train_sft_config.py: new TestEvalOnlyMode class (6 tests) — CLI flag, sft_kwargs safety, end-to-end mock with no train/save calls, over-limit failure path, ordering invariant (audit before model load).
